title: Nix & NixOS Reference

ast.value: "Nix Cheatsheet" "NixOS Cheatsheet" "Nix Reference"
Nix & NixOS Reference
reference public nix nixos reference cheatsheet devtools

ast: A dense reference for developers who have Nix exposure but keep forgetting the syntax. Not a tutorial.

ast.value:
# Build a flake output
nix build .#packageName
nix build github:owner/repo#packageName

# Run without installing
nix run .#appName -- --arg1 --arg2
nix run nixpkgs#ripgrep -- --help

# Enter dev shell
nix develop
nix develop .#shellName
nix develop --command bash -c "cargo build"

# Build old-style default.nix
nix-build -A packageName

# Show flake outputs
nix flake show
nix flake show github:owner/repo

# Update all inputs
nix flake update

# Update specific input
nix flake update nixpkgs

# Lock (write lock file without updating)
nix flake lock

# Check flake for errors
nix flake check

# Metadata about inputs
nix flake metadata

# Install a package
nix profile install nixpkgs#ripgrep

# List installed packages
nix profile list

# Remove a package (by index from list)
nix profile remove 3

# Upgrade all
nix profile upgrade '.*'

# Rollback
nix profile rollback

# Query what built a store path
nix-store --query --deriver /nix/store/abc123-package

# Show runtime dependencies
nix-store --query --requisites /nix/store/abc123-package

# Verify store integrity
nix-store --verify --check-contents

# Collect garbage (delete unreferenced store paths)
nix-collect-garbage
nix-collect-garbage -d          # also delete old profiles/generations
nix-collect-garbage --dry-run   # preview what would be deleted
nix-collect-garbage --delete-older-than 30d

# New CLI equivalent
nix store gc
nix store gc --dry-run
buildInputs

ast: Runtime deps; libraries linked at runtime

ast.value: nativeBuildInputs

ast: Build-time tools (compilers, pkg-config, cmake); cross-compile safe

ast.value: propagatedBuildInputs

ast: package also need at build time

ast.value: propagatedNativeBuildInputs

ast: : If it's a tool you run (compiler, linker, code gen), use

ast.value: nativeBuildInputs

ast: . If it's a library you link against, use

ast.value: buildInputs

pkgs.ripgrep.override { pcre2Support = true; }
pkgs.hello.override { stdenv = pkgs.clangStdenv; }
builtins.toString
builtins.toJSON
builtins.fromJSON

ast.value: builtins.pathExists

# CLI search
nix search nixpkgs#ripgrep
nix search nixpkgs# "json parser"

# Website (much faster)
# https://search.nixos.org/packages
# https://search.nixos.org/options

# Find attribute name for a binary (requires nix-index)
nix-locate --whole-name bin/ripgrep
nix-index   # build index first (one-time, slow ~10min)
nix-locate bin/somecommand

ast: Flakes evaluate in pure mode by default: no network, no files outside the flake,

ast.value: builtins.currentSystem

nix build --impure
nix eval --impure .#something

# Don't use builtins.currentSystem in pure flakes
# Use: flake-utils.lib.eachDefaultSystem (system: { ... })

title: Why Your Binary Is Not in PATH

ast.value: buildInputs
nativeBuildInputs
nix develop

ast: Occurs when a store path is used as a Nix source file during evaluation. Forces a build during eval, slow, breaks pure evaluation.

ast.value:
# BAD: IFD
let result = import (pkgs.runCommand "gen" {} "echo 42 > $out"); in result
# Prefer generating values at eval time
buildInputs
nativeBuildInputs
nativeBuildInputs

nativeBuildInputs = [ pkgs.pkg-config pkgs.cmake pkgs.rustc pkgs.cargo ];  # tools
buildInputs = [ pkgs.openssl pkgs.libiconv ];                               # linked libs

src = ./my-dir;      # Nix path: copied into /nix/store/hash-my-dir
src = "./my-dir";   # String: NOT copied - usually wrong for src
configFile = toString ./config.toml;  # Path to string without store copy

nix repl

:l <nixpkgs>              # load nixpkgs from NIX_PATH
:lf .                     # load flake from current dir
pkgs = import <nixpkgs> {} # instantiate
pkgs.git                  # inspect package
pkgs.git.meta             # inspect metadata
:e pkgs.git               # open source in $EDITOR
:q                        # quit

# After :lf .
outputs.devShells.x86_64-linux.default
outputs.packages.x86_64-linux

nix eval .#packages.x86_64-linux.default.name
nix eval nixpkgs#git.version
nix eval --expr '1 + 1'
nix eval --json .#packages.x86_64-linux.default.meta
nix eval --raw nixpkgs#git.outPath

nix build --show-trace          # full evaluation stack trace
nix build --print-build-logs    # build logs
nix build -L                    # shorthand
nix build -vv                   # verbose
nix build --keep-failed         # keep failed build dir
nix log /nix/store/hash-package # show stored build log

sudo nixos-rebuild switch --flake .#myhostname
sudo nixos-rebuild test    # temporary switch
sudo nixos-rebuild boot    # apply on next boot
sudo nixos-rebuild switch --rollback

home-manager switch --flake .#"alice@myhostname"
home-manager switch --flake .#alice

# As NixOS module - rebuilds alongside system
sudo nixos-rebuild switch --flake .#hostname

ast.raw-value: systemd User Services via Home Manager
title: systemd User Services via Home Manager

ast.value:
# Run a package without installing
nix run nixpkgs#cowsay -- "Hello, Nix!"

# Temporary shell with packages
nix shell nixpkgs#git nixpkgs#gh
nix-shell -p git gh              # old-style

# Show store path
nix eval --raw nixpkgs#git.outPath

# Trace what built a binary
nix-store --query --deriver $(which git)

# Check flake without building
nix flake check --no-build

# Format all .nix files
nix run nixpkgs#alejandra -- .

# Current nixpkgs revision
nix flake metadata nixpkgs | grep Revision

# Open build environment
nix develop nixpkgs#git

# List NixOS generations
nix-env --list-generations --profile /nix/var/nix/profiles/system

# Closure size
nix path-info -S nixpkgs#chromium

# Why does my system need openssl?
nix why-depends /run/current-system nixpkgs#openssl

# Copy store path to remote
nix copy --to ssh://user@host /nix/store/hash-package

# Derivation details
nix show-derivation nixpkgs#git

ast: When the hash is unknown, use a fake SRI hash - Nix will error with the correct one:

ast.value:
# Prefetch helpers
nix-prefetch-url --unpack https://github.com/owner/repo/archive/v1.2.3.tar.gz
nix-prefetch-git https://github.com/owner/repo.git --rev v1.2.3
